strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, exceeding the 15-year minimum
Entrepreneurial track record: founded and still runs GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform with real-world adoption (Green Map)
Recent AI-adjacent work at re:cinq on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure, plus a history of public talks and workshops
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth — no AI-related advanced degree, no recognized AI products or platforms, and AI experience is limited to ~1 year of agent tooling
Career trajectory is senior/staff IC on product teams; no evidence of director-level scope, strategic advisory to customers, or org-wide technical leadership
Cloud experience appears limited to application-level usage (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys) rather than cloud platform strategy or architecture leadership

key_factors.factor: AI expertise gap vs. role's core focus
Long, broad engineering experience with entrepreneurial initiative

response.justification: The candidate comfortably meets the minimum bar — 15+ years of engineering experience and a technical bachelor's degree — and shows genuine builder credentials, open-source founding experience, and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure. However, this role is a director-level AI strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products, and the ability to advise strategic customers and shape cloud product direction. The resume reflects a strong senior/staff full-stack engineer, not an established AI leader, making the fit weak for the role's central mandate despite solid general engineering credentials.
